title: ProvenanceEvent
description: One record in the hardened append-only provenance ledger, per D19. The shape is exactly {schema_version, run_id, protocol_version, event_id, ts, type, payload, source_response_hashes, versions}; all nine are required and no other top-level field is permitted. SQLite transactions are the primary store (Windows-friendly, safe under concurrent writers); JSONL is an export format, not the write path. The ledger is append-only: no update or delete path exists, and PRISMA flow counts are DERIVED by aggregating events (`provenance prisma`), never stored as a mutable shape (D10). 'ts' is caller-supplied and never self-generated, so a replay reproduces the ledger byte for byte (D15).

schema_version.description: Version of this event schema, so a ledger written by an older core stays readable.

run_id.description: Groups every event of one logical run. The join key for aggregation, PRISMA derivation, and lineage queries.

protocol_version.description: Pins the decision rules in force when the event was written (the D9 precedence, the axis thresholds), so a verdict is never read under rules it was not produced by.

event_id.description: Unique identifier of this event within the ledger. Any collision-resistant string (UUID or content hash); uniqueness is enforced by the store.

ts.description: RFC 3339 event timestamp, CALLER-SUPPLIED and never self-generated. Self-generated timestamps would make replays non-deterministic, which D15 forbids.

payload.description: Type-specific event body. Deliberately open: each event type carries its own fields (a retrieval carries the query and the source; a dedup_decision carries the winner, the losers, and the similarity that drove the merge; a record_lineage carries the derived artifact and the records it came from). Payload keys are canonically ordered on write so JSONL export is byte-stable.

source_response_hashes.description: Hashes of the snapshots this event consumed, joining the ledger to the snapshot store (snapshot.schema.json response_hash). Required, and empty ONLY for events that consumed no source response at all (for example a gate decision over already-derived artifacts). This is the tie that makes an event auditable back to raw bytes.

versions.description: The component versions in force. With the snapshot and the configuration, these are the three things D15 requires for a byte-identical replay.

parser.description: Parser version; an input to every passage ID, so it must be recorded alongside any event that touches extracted text.

model.description: Model identifier, present only when a model was involved in producing the event. Absent for purely deterministic events.

eventType.description: Closed event vocabulary per D10 and D19. M2 emits retrieval, record_lineage, and dedup_decision; M3 adds the compile-gate events (artifact_hash, gate, review); M4 adds the protocol and screening lifecycle (protocol_locked, amendment, screening_decision, adjudication).

contentHash.description: SHA-256 content hash, hex encoded, optionally prefixed with 'sha256:'.
